text.title: SLO Definition — Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes when authoring SLOs per class. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Dashboard-driven SLOs: targets copied from current Grafana lines without a user-acceptable conversation.
failure-mode.detector: SLO target value matches the trailing-30-day baseline value ±0.05 AND user_acceptable_error_rationale field is empty or generic
failure-mode.repair: Hold the product conversation. Capture the rationale in writing. Re-set the target based on user acceptability; document the gap (or congruence) with current performance.

failure-mode.description: Missing budget policy: SLO exists but no document defines what burn means operationally. Burn alerts are noise.
failure-mode.detector: budget_policy_path empty OR document does not exist
failure-mode.repair: Write the policy. Even a 1-page minimum: who owns the SLO, what triggers a freeze, what reverts it. The SLO is not in production until the policy is published.

failure-mode.description: Class mismatch: service classified as API but is actually an async worker masked behind an API. Template SLIs miss the queue-age signal that matters.
failure-mode.detector: API SLI set in use AND service has a backlog metric showing >1min queue age routinely
failure-mode.repair: Re-classify the service. Apply the async-worker template. Document the class change.

failure-mode.description: SLI sprawl: 9 SLIs per service; alerts everywhere; nobody owns the response.
failure-mode.repair: Cut to template + 2. Move discarded metrics to monitoring dashboards (informational) but not SLI status. Document the rationale.

failure-mode.description: Calendar-month window leak: SLO is computed monthly; end-of-month sees panic and Jan-1 sees an artificial reset that hides drift.
failure-mode.detector: window field equals "calendar-month" OR cron-driven monthly resets exist in the metric pipeline
failure-mode.repair: Switch to 30-day rolling window. Update the metric query and alerting rules. Communicate the change to stakeholders.

failure-mode.description: Stale review: SLO defined 18 months ago; service has 10x more traffic and 2 new dependencies; target is no longer meaningful.
failure-mode.detector: last_reviewed older than 90 days OR traffic volume changed > 3x since last review
failure-mode.repair: Quarterly SLO review with product. Update target or window as needed. Spawn ADR if architecture changed substantially.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: SLO scaffolding agents read service code and Grafana dashboards. They MUST NOT propose a target based on the current dashboard value alone. The user-acceptable rationale comes from product; the agent suggests, the product owner approves.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Product owner co-signs SLO targets. SRE / DevOps owns the SLI definitions and the budget policy. The 90-day review forces fresh conversation rather than passive carry-over.
